2604.24176.title: Explanation Quality Assessment as Ranking with Listwise Rewards
2604.24176.paper_summary: We reformulate explanation quality assessment as a ranking problem rather than a generation problem. Instead of optimizing models to produce a single "best" explanation token-by-token, we train reward models to discriminate among multiple candidate explanations and learn their relative quality. Concretely, we construct per-instance candidate sets with graded quality levels and train listwise and pairwise ranking models (ListNet, LambdaRank, RankNet) to preserve ordinal structure and avoid score compression typical of pointwise regression or binary preference objectives. We observe three findings: First, ranking losses consistently outperform regression on score separation across all domains tested. Second, the optimal ranking loss depends on data characteristics: listwise objectives excel with well-separated quality tiers, while pairwise methods are more robust to noisy natural annotations. Third, when trained on carefully curated and well-structured data, small encoder models can match models that are orders of magnitude larger, suggesting that data quality matters more than model scale. Finally, when used as rewards in policy optimization, ranking-based scores enable stable convergence in settings where regression-based rewards fail entirely. Code and data are available at: https://github.com/Tankiit/PPO_Learning_to_rank
2604.24176.paper_summary_zh: 我們將解釋質量評估重新表述為排序問題，而不是生成問題。<lb/>我們不再優化模型以逐字生成單一的「最佳」解釋，而是訓練獎勵模型來區分多個候選解釋並學習它們的相對質量。<lb/>具體來說，我們構建了每個實例的候選集，並設置了分級質量水平，然後訓練列表式和成對排序模型（ListNet、LambdaRank、RankNet），以保持序數結構並避免點對點回歸或二元偏好目標中典型的分數壓縮。<lb/>我們觀察到三個發現：首先，排序損失在所有測試的領域中，始終優於回歸在分數分離上的表現。<lb/>其次，最佳的排序損失取決於數據特徵：列表式目標在質量層次明顯分離時表現出色，而成對方法對噪聲自然標註則更具穩健性。<lb/>第三，當在經過精心策劃和結構良好的數據上訓練時，小型編碼器模型可以匹配規模大幾個量級的模型，這表明數據質量比模型規模更為重要。<lb/>最後，當用作政策優化中的獎勵時，基於排序的分數能夠在回歸基礎的獎勵完全失敗的情況下實現穩定收斂。<lb/>代碼和數據可在以下網址獲得：https://github.com/Tankiit/PPO_Learning_to_rank
2604.24176.author: Thomas Bailleux et.al.
2604.24176.authors: Thomas Bailleux, Tanmoy Mukherjee, Emmanuel Lonca, Pierre Marquis, Zied Bouraoui
